ground_truth.title: 2025 Best Value Award
ground_truth.author: Marc Phillips
ground_truth.main_content:
The pt.AUDIO Best Value Award illustrates, year after year, that there’s more to this category than mere affordability. I think when we first envisioned the Best Value category, we wanted to highlight inexpensive hi-fi gear that “punched above its weight,” the kind of equipment that would attract new people to the hobby before they were discouraged by the inevitable long-term sticker shock. For the last couple of years, just around the time we decided to pursue more upscale gear in our formal reviews, we’ve seen a distinct split between inexpensive audio products and more expensive products that nonetheless accomplish far more than the competition. For the 2025 Best Value Award nominations, the principle of “value” is more more complex than ever–even though nothing in high-end audio beats a screaming deal. But there’s another side to the 2025 Best Value Award, and that’s not charging a premium for a paradigm shift. It happened a lot this year.

Here are the ten nominees for the 2025 Best Value Award from pt.AUDIO (in alphabetical order):

Audio by Van Alstine NP-1 Power Amplifier

This unassuming little $1,200 class AB power amp might not be a flashy or exciting way to start off our 2025 Best Value Award list, but it has one thing going for it–it was designed by the legendary Frank Van Alstine. In fact, the NP-1 might be the last product to be designed from Frank before he died earlier this year (unless there’s something in the pipeline, especially since the company was sold to new owners). That makes the Audio by Van Alstine NP-1 power amplifier a bittersweet yet obvious choice for the list. The NP-1 not only defines the very idea of value, but it qualifies as the most perfect “workhorse” power amp we’ve seen:

“It’s an inexpensive stereo power amplifier that will elevate a modest system, but it’s also so superb and practical that it stands up to upgrade fever. As I found out, it can excel in very ambitious systems without attracting attention to itself–which means it would make an excellent workhorse amplifier. What the NP1 won’t do is give you that same lushness and warmth of a classic tube amplifier, and I get that. At the same time, I love hearing the very different perspective of the NP1. It delivers so much detail and yet it never sounded harsh, brittle or busy in its tonality.”

Fender X MoFi American Vintage Turntable

At first glance the American Vintage turntable design seems to focus on great looks, based on a specific musical theme. Once we started to listen to this affordable, nearly plug-and-play gem, we immediately noticed how much sonic goodness it offers for a price that starts at just $2,999 with arm. (We opted for the $1,495 MoFi MC Gold cartridge which lifts the total price to just $3,999.) It’s the rare analog rig that will appeal to newbies, collectors and audiophiles  equally.

“In fact, I started to regard the Fender x MoFi American Vintage deck as a perfect source for producing music that is simply beautiful in nature. Can a lovely pastel-sounding turntable sweep you off your feet while providing all that objective musical information that audiophiles crave? In this case, the answer is yes.”
